_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/ DNB equivalent (in Urology/ Pediatric Surgery)
clinical_indications: Symptoms from obstruction, such as recurrent flank pain, nausea, and vomiting

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Was the clinical notes and Intravenous pyelogram (IVP) /CT/CT-IVP/DTPA Renal scan report are indicative of procedure?

common_queries: Clinical notes and Intravenous pyelogram (IVP) /CT/CT-IVP/DTPA Renal scan report are not indicative of the procedure.
Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment and advice for admission not submitted at pre-authorization.
Intravenous pyelogram (IVP) /CT/CT-IVP/DTPA Renal scan report not submitted at pre-authorization.
Intra procedure clinical photograph not submitted at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Pyeloplasty: A surgical reconstruction of renal pelvis, to drain and decompress renal pelvis due to Uretero-pelvic junction obstruction (UPJ).
Pyeloplasty: If an indwelling ureteral stent is left in place, it is usually removed cystoscopically 4-6 weeks after the procedure.
Pyeloplasty: Generally, perform imaging studies approximately 6-8 weeks after the procedure to assess renal function and to help rule out residual obstruction.

Pyeloureterostomy: reconnecting the donor ureter to either the recipient ureter (ureteroureterostomy) or bladder (ureteroneocystostomy), or creating an anastomosis between the renal pelvis and recipient native ureter.

Pyelopyelostomy: Intrinsic defect at the uretero-pelvic junction (UPJ) is not necessarily present and so, dismembered pyeloplasty would not be necessary. laparoscopic transposition pyelo-pyelostomy (LTP) to treat UPJO.
